input.description:
Compose `FieldsContext` into `KanbanContext`, provide `kanban_defaults()`, and update the kanban init flow.

**1. Add `kanban_defaults()` function**

New file or module in swissarmyhammer-kanban providing the built-in field and entity definitions:

```rust
fn kanban_defaults() -> FieldDefaults {
    FieldDefaults::new()
        // Task fields
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000TITLE00"), name: "title", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000STATUS0"), name: "status", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000PRIORTY"), name: "priority", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK00000000000TAGS00"), name: "tags", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000ASSIGN0"), name: "assignee", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000DUEDAT0"), name: "due", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK00000000000BODY00"), name: "description", ... })
        // Tag fields
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG000000000000ID000"), name: "tag_name", validate: Some(...), ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000COLOR00"), name: "color", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000DESC000"), name: "description", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000USAGE00"), name: "usage", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000LAST000"), name: "last_used", ... })
        // Entity templates
        .entity(EntityDef { name: "task", body_field: Some("description"), fields: vec![...] })
        .entity(EntityDef { name: "tag", body_field: Some("description"), fields: vec![...] })
}
```

**2. Compose FieldsContext into KanbanContext**

Update `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`:
- Add `fields: FieldsContext` field to `KanbanContext`
- Update `open()` / initialization to create FieldsContext at `root.join("fields")`
- Call `.with_defaults(kanban_defaults())`
- Expose `pub fn fields(&self) -> &FieldsContext`

**3. Update init command**

When `init board` is called:
- FieldsContext is already initialized via `open()` + `with_defaults()`
- The `fields/` directory is created and populated automatically
- No changes needed to the init command itself â€” the composition handles it

**Files:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (new), `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` (add swissarmyhammer-fields dep)

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Add swissarmyhammer-fields dependency to swissarmyhammer-kanban
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with all built-in fields from architecture doc
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with task and tag entity templates
- [ ] Add FieldsContext field to KanbanContext
- [ ] Update KanbanContext::open() to compose FieldsContext
- [ ] Expose fields() accessor method
- [ ] Write test: init creates fields/ directory with defaults
- [ ] Write test: fields are accessible via context.fields()
- [ ] Write test: re-open preserves user customizations
- [ ] Verify all existing kanban tests still pass
input.title: Integrate FieldsContext into KanbanContext

Compose `FieldsContext` into `KanbanContext`, provide `kanban_defaults()`, and update the kanban init flow.

**1. Add `kanban_defaults()` function**

New file or module in swissarmyhammer-kanban providing the built-in field and entity definitions:

```rust
fn kanban_defaults() -> FieldDefaults {
    FieldDefaults::new()
        // Task fields
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000TITLE00"), name: "title", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000STATUS0"), name: "status", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000PRIORTY"), name: "priority", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK00000000000TAGS00"), name: "tags", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000ASSIGN0"), name: "assignee", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK0000000000DUEDAT0"), name: "due", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTASK00000000000BODY00"), name: "description", ... })
        // Tag fields
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG000000000000ID000"), name: "tag_name", validate: Some(...), ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000COLOR00"), name: "color", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000DESC000"), name: "description", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000USAGE00"), name: "usage", ... })
        .field(FieldDef { id: ulid!("01JMTAG00000000000LAST000"), name: "last_used", ... })
        // Entity templates

        .entity(EntityDef { name: "tag", body_field: Some("description"), fields: vec![...] })
}
```

**2. Compose FieldsContext into KanbanContext**

Update `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`:
- Add `fields: FieldsContext` field to `KanbanContext`
- Update `open()` / initialization to create FieldsContext at `root.join("fields")`
- Call `.with_defaults(kanban_defaults())`
- Expose `pub fn fields(&self) -> &FieldsContext`

**3. Update init command**

When `init board` is called:
- FieldsContext is already initialized via `open()` + `with_defaults()`
- The `fields/` directory is created and populated automatically
- No changes needed to the init command itself â€” the composition handles it

**Files:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs` (new), `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/context.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs`, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/Cargo.toml` (add swissarmyhammer-fields dep)

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Add swissarmyhammer-fields dependency to swissarmyhammer-kanban
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with all built-in fields from architecture doc
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with task and tag entity templates
- [ ] Add FieldsContext field to KanbanContext
- [ ] Update KanbanContext::open() to compose FieldsContext
- [ ] Expose fields() accessor method
- [ ] Write test: init creates fields/ directory with defaults
- [ ] Write test: fields are accessible via context.fields()
- [ ] Write test: re-open preserves user customizations
- [ ] Verify all existing kanban tests still pass
output.title: Integrate FieldsContext into KanbanContext

input.description:
Compose `FieldsContext` into `KanbanContext`, provide `kanban_defaults()`.

**kanban_defaults() â€” ALL kanban entities:**
- **Task fields:** title, status, priority, tags (computed), assignees (reference, multiple), due, depends_on (reference, multiple), body
- **Tag fields:** tag_name (with validation), color, description, usage (computed), last_used (computed)
- **Shared fields:** name (text), order (number), actor_type (select: human/agent)
- **Entity templates:** task (body_field: body), tag (no body_field), actor, column, swimlane

**KanbanContext changes:**
- Add `fields: FieldsContext` field
- Update open() to create FieldsContext at `root.join("fields")` with `kanban_defaults()`
- Expose `pub fn fields(&self) -> &FieldsContext`
- Implement `EntityLookup` for kanban stores (dispatches on entity_type to check tasks/, tags/, actors/, etc.)

**Subtasks:**
- [ ] Add swissarmyhammer-fields dependency to swissarmyhammer-kanban
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with all built-in fields
- [ ] Implement kanban_defaults() with all 5 entity templates (task, tag, actor, column, swimlane)
- [ ] Implement EntityLookup for kanban stores
- [ ] Add FieldsContext field to KanbanContext
- [ ] Update KanbanContext::open() to compose FieldsContext
- [ ] Expose fields() accessor
- [ ] Write test: init creates fields/ directory with defaults
- [ ] Write test: fields are accessible via context.fields()
- [ ] Write test: re-open preserves user customizations
- [ ] Verify all existing kanban tests still pass
